Incorrect RCA lines

NOAA summary

Self-intersections (points out of order)

The table below includes all RCA lines that, extracted from the CSV files coordinates, present some points out of order. Thus, resulting in incorrect shapes. Scroll down to see all the lines.

Points inland

Here we determine whether some of the nodes are wrongly located inland. For this process, we have used shoreline intersection as a proxy. Therefore, a selected line does not necessarily mean it has a point on land, and visual inspection is required. Further, lines whose ending points were situated on land were discarded since line endings near the shore were seen to be relatively close to the coastline and should not substantially affect the definition of RCAs. Instead, they may be caused by a problem of shoreline resolution.

As seen in the table and maps below, the only line affected by points located on land is 60fm_010104.csv. Zooming out reveals that a node is located north of Ventura. As for 75fm_012703.csv, the script detected it only due to the shoreline intersection proxy, as the incorrect shape previously identified crosses land areas.

boundary_name area_name land_point lon_lat
60fm_010104.csv 60-fm (110-m) Channel Islands - Northern TRUE (-119.34025897202 34.2935415045793),(-119.3566667 34.3097293347648)
